name: Joystick Module
fullName: Dual-Axis Joystick Module with Push Button
tagline: Two-axis analog joystick with built-in push button for directional control
description: The joystick module is a dual-axis analog input device that works like the thumbstick on a game controller. It uses two potentiometers to output X and Y axis values (0–1023) and includes a push button activated by pressing down. Perfect for robots, RC cars, camera gimbals, and game controllers in Arduino projects.

pinout.name: X-Axis
pinout.desc: Analog output for X-axis (connect to A0)

pinout.name: Y-Axis
pinout.desc: Analog output for Y-axis (connect to A1)

wiringNote: Connect VRx and VRy to analog pins. Use INPUT_PULLUP for the SW pin so the button reads HIGH at rest and LOW when pressed.
libraryInstall: No installation required. Use analogRead() for axes and digitalRead() for the button.
commonMistakes: Forgetting INPUT_PULLUP on SW pin → floating/noisy button reads
Not centering the joystick deadzone in code → robot drifts at rest
Connecting SW to an analog pin instead of a digital pin
